mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing starts above 1.2 million units signals a surge in demand for capital goods, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with HOUST > 1.2M are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or HOUST fails to exceed 1.2M in 60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production below 105 signals a supply-side shock that compresses profit margins, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with INDPRO < 105 are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or INDPRO fails to decline below 105 in 60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in initial unemployment claims above 250K signals a surge in labor market uncertainty, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with PAYEMS > 250K are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or PAYEMS fails to exceed 250K in 60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the federal funds rate above 2.5% signals a tightening monetary cycle that compresses equity valuations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with FEDFUNDS > 2.5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or FEDFUNDS fails to exceed 2.5% in 60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real GDP above 2.5% signals a surge in demand for capital goods, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with GDPC1 > 2.5% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or GDPC1 fails to exceed 2.5% in 60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production below 100 signals a supply-side shock that compresses profit margins, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with INDPRO < 100 are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or INDPRO fails to decline below 100 in 60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in initial unemployment claims above 200K and a 3-month decline in real GDP below 2% signals a surge in labor market uncertainty and a decline in demand, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with PAYEMS > 200K and GDPC1 < 2% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or PAYEMS fails to exceed 200K or GDPC1 fails to decline below 2% in 60% of instances.
